title: System for tissue graft anchoring
abstract: A device for securing soft tissue includes a suture construct having a first end and a second end, a braided tubular portion formed from yarn and defining a lumen, the lumen being open at the first end, and a non-braided portion formed from the yarn at the second end. The non-braided portion forms a loop at the second end. A method of securing a graft includes placing a graft through the loop of the suture construct, pulling on the suture construct to locate the loop and graft in a bone tunnel, and placing a locking device within the lumen of the suture construct to secure the suture construct and graft in the bone tunnel.
claims: Claims CLAIMS  What is claimed is: 1. A graft fixation device for securing soft tissue to bone, the device comprising: a suture construct comprising: a first end and a second end; a braided tubular portion formed from yarn and defining a lumen, the lumen being open at the first end; a non-braided portion at the second end, the non-braided portion extending from the braided tubular portion and being formed from the yarn, the non-braided portion forming a loop at the second end; and a locking device sized for receipt within the lumen of the braided tubular portion. 2. The device of claim 1 wherein the locking device includes surface features. 3. The device of claim 2 wherein the surface features are threads. 4. The device of claim 2 wherein the surface features are spiked tips. 5. The device of claim 1 wherein the locking device is formed from suture. 6. The device of claim 1 wherein the locking device includes pointed ends. 7. The device of claim 1 wherein a long dimension of at least a portion of the locking device is about 4 mm. 8. The device of claim 1 wherein a diameter of the braided tubular portion is about 5 mm to about 12 mm. 9. The device of claim 1 wherein the locking device comprises at least one suture receiving hole. 10. The device of claim 1 further comprising at least one suture attached to the locking device. 11. A method of securing a graft in a bone tunnel, the method comprising: forming a bone tunnel in a bone; placing a graft through a loop of a suture construct; pulling on the suture construct to locate the loop and graft in the bone tunnel; and placing a locking device within a lumen of the suture construct to secure the suture construct and graft in the bone tunnel. 12. The method of claim 11 wherein placing the graft comprising placing the graft through a non-braided portion of the suture construct. 13. The method of claim 11 wherein placing the locking device comprising placing the locking device within a braided tubular portion of the suture construct. 14. The method of claim 11 wherein the bone tunnel comprises a first portion extending through cortical bone and a second portion passing through cancellous bone, a diameter of the first portion selected to the smaller than a diameter of the second portion. 15. The method of claim 11 wherein a long dimension of at least a portion of the locking device is selected to be larger than the diameter of the first portion of the bone tunnel. 16. The device of claim 11 wherein a long dimension of at least a portion of the locking device is about 4 mm. 17. The method of claim 11 wherein a diameter of the first portion of the bone tunnel is about 2.4 mm. 18. The method of claim 11 wherein a diameter of the second portion of the bone tunnel is between about 2.5 and 4.5 mm. 19. The method of claim 11 wherein pulling on the suture construct to locate the loop and graft in a bone tunnel comprises pulling on a suture attached to the suture construct through the bone tunnel. 20. The method of claim 11 wherein placing a locking device within a lumen of the suture construct comprises pulling on a suture attached to the locking device through the lumen of the suture construct.
